title: How Lenders Underwrite Your Deal, and How to Get to Yes Faster

blocks.text: A look inside the credit process from people who ran it — and the file quality that moves approvals from weeks to days. Our team spent decades on the other side of the desk; this is how the decision actually gets made.

items: Origination screens for fit with the lender's current appetite
Underwriting rebuilds your numbers from source documents
Credit weighs the deal against policy, precedent, and portfolio exposure
Committee signs off — or sends questions back down the chain

blocks.text: Every loop back down that chain costs one to two weeks. The files that move fastest are the ones that never generate a question: source documents attached, assumptions defended, weaknesses disclosed with mitigations already in place.

It means we underwrite your deal the way the lender will — same metrics, same stress tests, same policy lenses — before anyone sees it. When the file lands, the analyst's work is already done, and the answer comes back faster because there's nothing left to chase.
